evals.prompt: Use the doca-flow-dpa-perf skill to help me. I'm working on: invoking doca_flow_dpa_perf on DPA-capable hardware (ConnectX-7+ or BlueField-3) to measure rule update / disable rates on the DPA-offloaded DOCA Flow path — picking the active / passive device split, choosing workload-shape axes (burst, queue, completion threshold, workers, hash pipe algo, PSL tables), or reading Kops/sec iteration stats and the optional self-test.
evals.expected_output: The agent selects the doca-flow-dpa-perf skill and answers from its documented workflow, quoting only real symbols/commands from the bundle (no invented tokens).
assertions: The doca-flow-dpa-perf skill is selected and its SKILL.md/TASKS.md drive the answer.
Every symbol, flag, or command named is traceable to the bundle (no invented tokens).

evals.prompt: I'm running into this: "how fast can the DPA program path-selector entries". Can you walk me through it?
evals.expected_output: The agent recognizes this as a doca-flow-dpa-perf issue even though the skill is not named, loads it, and answers grounded in the bundle.
assertions: The doca-flow-dpa-perf skill is triggered from the implicit phrasing without the user naming it.
The answer stays grounded in real bundle symbols/commands.

evals.prompt: I'm running into this: "baseline rule-update rate on ConnectX-8". Can you walk me through it?
evals.expected_output: The agent recognizes this as a doca-flow-dpa-perf issue even though the skill is not named, loads it, and answers grounded in the bundle.
assertions: The doca-flow-dpa-perf skill is triggered from the implicit phrasing without the user naming it.
The answer stays grounded in real bundle symbols/commands.

evals.prompt: I actually need help with the host / DPU-CPU Flow path. Which skill should handle this, and why is it not doca-flow-dpa-perf?
evals.expected_output: The agent does NOT force doca-flow-dpa-perf; it explains that the host / DPU-CPU Flow path is out of doca-flow-dpa-perf's scope and routes to the appropriate skill instead.
assertions: doca-flow-dpa-perf is not selected as the primary skill for this out-of-scope request.
The answer routes to the appropriate skill(s) instead.
